reasoning: Satellite imagery showed clear cloud formation patterns consistent with historical rain events. Pressure drop of 8mb in 6 hours.
lesson: Satellite + pressure data combination is reliable for 12-24h forecasts

reasoning: Coastal breeze pattern suggested moderation. Historical data showed 78% of similar patterns resulted in temps below threshold.
lesson: Weight real-time wind data higher than historical patterns. 6-hour timing errors are decisive.

reasoning: Jet stream dip + lake effect moisture = high confidence. Multiple model convergence on accumulation totals.
lesson: Multi-model convergence on jet stream patterns is highly reliable

reasoning: Gulf stream warming pushes humidity to 78-82% range. March avg is 72%.
Cold front arriving midday. Models showed max temp 4°F below threshold.
lesson: Cold front timing within 6-hour window is predictable

reasoning: Pacific storm track looked certain. Moisture plume clearly visible.
lesson: Storm track can shift 50+ miles in final 6 hours. Need tighter confidence bands.

reasoning: Upslope flow + Arctic air mass. Classic Denver snow setup.
lesson: Upslope patterns are highly reliable when Arctic air is present

reasoning: Cloud cover from Pacific system should limit heating.
Nor'easter developing offshore. Precipitation shield expanding.
lesson: Nor'easter precipitation shields are predictable 18h out

reasoning: Atmospheric river event. Precipitation totals locked in.
lesson: Atmospheric rivers have high predictability 24h out

reasoning: High pressure should limit wind speeds.
Squall line approaching from west. Timing looked certain.
lesson: Squall line timing can vary ±2 hours. Build buffer into confidence.

reasoning: Marine layer depth increasing. Onshore flow persistent.
Warm air advection from south. Cold front stalled north.
lesson: Warm air advection can overcome cold patterns when front stalls

reasoning: Gulf moisture surge. Convergence along stationary front.
lesson: Gulf moisture surges are reliable when convergence present

reasoning: Trough deepening. Orographic lift increasing.
High pressure ridge blocking wind. Gradient too weak.
lesson: High pressure ridges are reliable wind blockers

reasoning: Warm front approaching. Precipitation shield expanding.
Desert high pressure breaking down. Cooler air from west.
lesson: Desert high pressure breakdown is visible 36h in advance

reasoning: Clipper system approaching. Lake enhancement possible.
